title: neurogramma pedata
parts: Neurogramma pedata has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which are utilized in traditional medicine. The leaves of Neurogramma pedata contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of this plant are rich in alkaloids, including neurogrammine and neurogramminine, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The flowers of Neurogramma pedata contain glycosides and anthocyanins, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as potential hepatoprotective effects.
preparations: Neurogramma pedata has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from Neurogramma pedata is often used to treat fever, colds, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The tincture of Neurogramma pedata is commonly used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, particularly for conditions like arthritis and muscle spasms. Additionally, the infusion of Neurogramma pedata is sometimes used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
side_effects: Neurogramma pedata can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive consumption leading to headaches, dizziness, and nausea. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause seizures, coma, and even death. Additionally, improper preparation and dosing can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems in some individuals. It is essential to consult a medical professional and follow established guidelines for safe use to minimize the risk of adverse side effects.
